goal: Pre-configure HA profile with AX650 voice stack auto-connect and Xiaomi Miot Auto integration
stack: Python adapters + Docker Compose + Home Assistant YAML config
validation_command: python -m pytest -q && docker-compose config && sh -n scripts/install_on_board.sh

requirement_alignment.goal: After `--with-homeassistant` profile starts, HA should auto-connect to AX650 voice stack (Wyoming STT/TTS/wakeword + OpenAI conversation) and have Xiaomi Miot Auto pre-installed for Xiaomi device control.
non_goals: Do not commit model artifacts or vendor caches.
Do not overwrite unrelated user changes.
Do not perform destructive board operations or automatically merge PR.
Do not include actual Xiaomi account credentials or tokens.
expected_behavior: `docker compose --profile homeassistant up -d` starts HA with AX650 voice stack auto-connected via configuration.yaml.
HA Assist Pipeline is pre-configured with AX650 STT/TTS/wakeword/local conversation.
Xiaomi Miot Auto is pre-installed via HACS for Xiaomi device discovery after user login.
Users only need to log into Xiaomi account in HA UI to start controlling devices.
README.md (Chinese) and README_EN.md are updated to document the Xiaomi integration.
constraints_and_risks: HA and voice stack share host network, connecting via 127.0.0.1.
Miot Auto requires Xiaomi account login; credentials are managed in HA UI, not in repo config.
HACS installation of Miot Auto may fail on fresh HA if HACS is not yet installed.
Board disk space: HA container + config may need additional space (~2GB).
acceptance_checks: `docker compose --profile homeassistant config` validates without errors.
HA configuration.yaml includes Wyoming integrations for ASR (10300), TTS (10200), openWakeWord (10400), and OpenAI conversation (8001).
HACS custom component config includes Xiaomi Miot Auto repository.
Assist Pipeline configuration references AX650 services.
All existing tests pass (pytest, compose config, shell syntax).
README files document Xiaomi integration setup flow.
requirement_alignment.validation_approach: Run existing pytest suite, validate compose config with homeassistant profile, shell syntax checks, HA YAML schema validation.
requirement_alignment.first_iteration_scope: Create homeassistant/config/ directory with configuration.yaml for Wyoming/OpenAI auto-connect and Assist Pipeline pre-config.

issues.title: feat[1]: pre-configure HA config with AX650 voice stack auto-connect
acceptance_checks: docker compose --profile homeassistant config validates without errors
configuration.yaml includes Wyoming STT integration pointing to 127.0.0.1:10300
configuration.yaml includes Wyoming TTS integration pointing to 127.0.0.1:10200
configuration.yaml includes openWakeWord integration pointing to 127.0.0.1:10400
configuration.yaml includes OpenAI conversation agent pointing to 127.0.0.1:8001
Assist Pipeline config references AX650 services
All existing tests pass (pytest, compose config, shell syntax)

rounds.implementation_summary: Created homeassistant/config/ with configuration.yaml that enables Wyoming auto-discovery for AX650 ASR/TTS/wakeword and configures OpenAI conversation agent for the AX650 LLM.

validation.evidence: All 9 structural checks passed (wyoming, openai_conversation, includes, etc.)

rounds.test_command: python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_install_on_board.py && sh -n scripts/install_on_board.sh && git diff --check
rounds.test_summary: All applicable tests pass. docker-compose not available on dev machine; compose config validation deferred to board-side smoke check.

assumptions: The HA profile uses network_mode: host, so all voice services are on 127.0.0.1.
HACS will be installed via docker-compose extra config or documented manual step.
Chinese README remains the primary documentation.
